name: chart-insufficient-data
title: Chart Insufficient Data
description: What a chart renders instead of a trend it cannot honestly draw — the count, the threshold, the period, and the readings themselves.

props.name: minimum
props.description: Readings this chart needs before a trend means anything. The caller's clinical judgement, not a library default.

props.name: period
props.description: The window the count covers — "30 days", "this admission".

props.name: children
props.description: The readings, listed rather than plotted. They are data — just not a trend.

clinicalNotes: A confident line drawn through two points is indistinguishable from a line through two hundred, and the reader has no way to detect the difference — this component is what a chart renders instead. It states the count, the threshold, and the period, and still shows the readings, because withholding them would be its own kind of dishonesty. `minimum` is deliberately required: how many readings constitute a trend is a clinical decision that varies by measure (two weights a year apart may be meaningful; two blood pressures on one morning are not), so the component will not invent a default.
notes: Render in place of the chart, not beside it. Composes empty-state.
